fix.description: ROOT CAUSE FOUND (not the SHA-1 — you added that correctly). The release build runs minifyEnabled=true but the ProGuard/R8 rules kept only Capacitor classes, so R8 obfuscated the Google Credential Manager classes (GetGoogleIdOption, GoogleIdTokenCredential, androidx.credentials). Play Services then could not vend a verifiable ID-token credential and Credential Manager returned status 16 ('[16] Account reauth failed'). This explains everything: it failed only on alpha/release (never debug), on BOTH sign-in styles, and was unaffected by the correct SHA-1. FIX (android/app/proguard-rules.pro, a44b7a26): added R8 keep rules for googleid + androidx.credentials(+playservices) + gms.auth.api.identity. VERIFIED in the v140 release mapping.txt — GetGoogleIdOption + the credential classes are now un-renamed (no longer stripped). Built + uploaded to Play ALPHA as v140 (1.6.82). TO TEST: update from the Play closed-testing (alpha) track to v140 and sign in with a fresh Gmail. TWO musts: (1) install FROM THE PLAY TRACK — not a sideloaded/locally-built APK; Play re-signs with the App-Signing cert A5:83:5C (the one registered + in google-services.json), while a sideloaded upload-key APK (B9:5C:EA) is on no OAuth client and will always [16]. (2) If it STILL fails on v140, in Cloud Console -> OAuth consent screen confirm User type = External (Internal/Workspace-only [16]s every personal Gmail regardless of SHA). Diagnosed via a 4-agent investigation workflow.

previousFix.description: Root cause of '[16] Account reauth failed': on Android, @capgo/capacitor-social-login routes through Credential Manager, which defaults filterByAuthorizedAccounts:true — it ONLY offers Google accounts that have ALREADY authorized this app. A first-time user (or a Family-Link / freshly-added account) has no authorized account, so Credential Manager returns NO credential and the SDK reports [16] reauth failed instead of showing a picker. Fix (v134, src/lib/google-auth.ts): force the bottom-sheet picker over ALL accounts on login -> options { style:'bottom', filterByAuthorizedAccounts:false, autoSelectEnabled:false }. This lets a brand-new user pick any Google account and authorize for the first time. tsc clean; shipped in versionCode 134 (1.6.76), built + uploaded to Play alpha. NOTE: native Google auth can't be reproduced in headless Playwright QA (proof image = clean Welcome render, build healthy, button present), so this needs a quick on-device test. If [16] still appears after v134 on a device, the remaining cause is Google Cloud Console OAuth config (the Android app's SHA-1 / package name not registered on the OAuth client) — that's owner-side; flag back and I'll walk through it.

judge.note: Code fix is solid + tsc-clean and the build renders cleanly (0 console errors), but the native Google account-picker cannot be exercised in headless QA, so on-device confirmation is still owner-side.

issues.description: Only 3 exercise not exercise video plan button, not building the workout plan correctly
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: the /ai/suggest-workout-day short-day top-up only fired 'if len(picked)<6 AND exclude_ids', so Day 1 (no sibling exclusions) was never topped up — the tier gate dropped the focus day to 3 (all push). Fix (backend a7a1053, deployed): top up ANY short day, re-selecting from the broader pre-gate pool + re-gating extras to the 6-movement target. Verified live: upper-body Day 1 now returns 6 balanced exercises (Push-Up/Bench/Pull-Up/Chin-Up/Spider Curl/Decline Press — push+pull+arms), not 3 all-press.

judge.note: Root-caused + deployed + verified LIVE on the real endpoint (3->6 exercises, breadth fixed). Proof is a faithful render of the live API response, which the onboarding builder renders verbatim (driving the deep mid-onboarding builder UI is impractical).

issues.description: Too much waiting here waited 1 minute, the should be less than 3 seconds and this is wrong screen we should have waited on the blurred screen after questionss
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: pre-build MealPrefsSheet makes the build read the ENRICHED meal-idea deck while the prefetch warmed only the BASELINE deck -> cache MISS -> the build looped empties SEQUENTIALLY (4 live suggestMealCached LLM calls ~= ~1 min). handleMealPrefsDone also dismissed the BuildFocusOverlay before building, so the wait happened on the bare builder with per-meal 'Coach Kabir is building...' spinners (the captured screen). Fix (CustomDietBuilder.tsx 323922b6): (1) concurrent build -- empties grouped by slot, slots run via Promise.all (~one LLM round-trip ~= ~15s instead of N); same-slot empties stay sequential within their slot so consumeMealIdea still drains distinct cards; each apply targets the meal's exact index (parallel-safe). (2) right screen -- handleMealPrefsDone keeps showFocus up; the blurred overlay shows 'Building your meals...' (CTAs disabled) and the build drops it only once the day is built. Repeat visits hit the warmed enriched deck -> instant.

judge.note: Both complaints fixed at root. Speed: sequential per-slot LLM loop (4x back-to-back ~1min, cache-miss on enriched deck) -> Promise.all across slots (~one round-trip ~15s); same-slot empties stay sequential within slot to keep distinct meals; apply targets exact meal index (parallel-safe). Screen: handleMealPrefsDone no longer dismisses the BuildFocusOverlay, so the wait now happens on the blurred screen showing 'Building your meals...' (the captured bare-builder screen is exactly what's eliminated). /ai/suggest-meal is not per-request rate limited so 4 concurrent is safe. tsc clean; 42 tests pass.

issues.description: Review everysingle.resolved from this bord and create a list of what not fixed make sure the latest code.is the update one don't compare discarded features with new features
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Reviewed every resolved issue (121: 118 archived + 3 recent) against the LATEST checked-out frontend + backend code via 8 parallel read-only agents, each citing concrete file:line/symbol evidence. Verified against current code (not historical commits); features later redesigned or removed on purpose were scored SUPERSEDED-OK, NOT broken (per your 'don't compare discarded features with new ones'). HEADLINE: 0 of 121 resolved issues are actually broken in the latest code (0 regressions, 109 KEPT-and-verified). The complete 'what's not fixed / can't-confirm' list = 12 items: (A) NEEDS A LOOK x1 -- e8cf 'add all FAQ of all features': the recorded resolution note (commit 18f9e94) is about social handles/LAUNCH_README, not the FAQ ask, BUT the FAQ feature itself exists (src/lib/faq-fallback.ts, 15 categories) -> bookkeeping mismatch only, no user-facing gap. (B) CANNOT-VERIFY FROM SOURCE x11 (none are regressions -- resolved outside the code tree or no fix recorded): OpenAI-billing-restored external = 2ce9, 9989, b4e3, 5d00; prod-DB/generated assets = b583 (thumbnails), f9e4 (coach portraits); empty fix desc / null commit = 463e, 9375, 0f79, 300e (meta board test); truncated note no tied commit = d039 (related toast fix b098 IS present). Recommend a live recheck on the OpenAI-dependent ones if you want certainty. Full per-issue evidence table retained in the audit run. No fix-loop code action required beyond optionally re-attaching e8cf's resolution note.

judge.note: Audit deliverable, not a code fix. All 121 resolved issues re-verified against the CURRENT frontend+backend source (8 parallel read-only agents, file:line evidence per issue). Honored the owner directive: checked latest code, scored intentionally-redesigned/removed features as SUPERSEDED-OK (not broken). Result: 0 regressions. The 'what's not fixed' list = 1 note-mismatch (e8cf, feature actually present) + 11 not-source-verifiable (OpenAI-billing/prod-data/empty-claim), enumerated with reasons.
